terms.definition: Bottom-up hierarchical clustering that starts with one cluster per point and repeatedly merges the closest pair of clusters.
Top-down hierarchical clustering that starts with all points in one cluster and recursively splits into smaller sub-clusters.
A tree diagram showing the order and dissimilarity at which clusters merge (or split) in hierarchical clustering.
A rule that defines the distance between two clusters from pairwise point distances (e.g. single, complete, average, Ward).
The merge height at which two original points first join the same cluster in an agglomerative dendrogram; measures how faithfully the tree preserves input distances.
